questions.questionText: Complete hydatidiform mole has a karyotype of:
questions.optionB: 46XX (entirely paternal origin — androgenetic, empty ovum fertilized by single sperm that duplicates, or two sperm)
questions.explanation: Complete mole: 46XX (90%) or 46XY — entirely paternal origin (androgenetic). Empty ovum fertilized by: (1) single sperm that duplicates (90%, 46XX), or (2) two sperm (dispermy, 46XX or 46XY). No fetal tissue, no amnion. Snowstorm appearance on USG. Very high β-hCG (>100,000). Theca lutein cysts. Risk of GTN: 15-20%. Partial mole: 69XXY/69XXX (triploid — 2 paternal + 1 maternal set). Has fetal tissue. Lower GTN risk (5%). Reference: DC Dutta — Molar Pregnancy.

questions.questionText: The most common cause of first trimester abortion is:
questions.optionB: Chromosomal abnormalities (most commonly autosomal trisomy — trisomy 16 is the most common)
questions.explanation: First trimester abortion: chromosomal abnormalities account for ~50-60% of all spontaneous abortions. Most common karyotypic abnormality: autosomal trisomy (52% of chromosomal causes). Trisomy 16: most common autosomal trisomy in abortuses (always lethal). Monosomy X (45XO): single most common chromosomal abnormality in abortuses overall. Types of abortion: threatened (bleeding, closed os), inevitable (bleeding, open os, intact products), incomplete (partial expulsion), complete, missed (dead fetus retained, closed os), septic. Reference: DC Dutta — Spontaneous Abortion.

questions.questionText: Placenta previa is classified as major when:
questions.optionB: Placenta covers or partially covers the internal os (Type III: marginal reaching os, Type IV: centralis completely covering os)
questions.explanation: Placenta previa: placenta implanted in lower uterine segment. Classification: Type I (low-lying — in lower segment but not reaching os), Type II (marginal — edge reaches os), Type III (partial — partially covers os), Type IV (complete/centralis — completely covers os). Types III & IV: major previa. Presentation: painless bright red vaginal bleeding in late pregnancy (after 28 weeks). NEVER do vaginal examination. Diagnosis: USG (TVS more accurate than TAS). Treatment: Type IV/major with bleeding → cesarean section. Reference: DC Dutta — Placenta Previa.
